get.description: Returns basic runtime information about the running server.
Streams 10 text chunks using chunked transfer encoding. For development/testing only.

200.description: A series of newline-delimited text chunks streamed to the client.

get.description: Streams package.json as a downloadable file. For development/testing only.

200.description: Returns package.json as a file attachment.

post.description: Accepts an image file upload and stores it. For development/testing only.

get.description: Returns authenticated user profile data from the access token context.

post.description: Creates a pending registration and sends OTP to the email. Frontend should call /register/verify after receiving OTP.
Verifies OTP and creates the account. Frontend should store returned authorization tokens after success.

400.description: Invalid or expired OTP, or registration creation failed.

post.summary: Step 2: Verify OTP and complete registration

post.description: Authenticates user with email/password and returns access + refresh tokens.

login_as.description: Select login audience for client-side flow. Authentication still validates credentials against the matched account.

post.description: Issues a new access token using an existing valid refresh token.
Revokes refresh token for current authenticated user.

patch.description: Updates profile fields for authenticated user. Avatar should be sent as multipart file.

post.description: Sends an OTP/token to email for password reset flow.
Verifies email using OTP/token sent to the user email.
Resends email verification OTP/token to the provided email.
Resets password using email + OTP/token + new password.
Changes password for currently authenticated user.
Accepts Google ID token from mobile client (Flutter/Android/iOS) and returns application auth payload.
Accepts Apple identity token from mobile client (Flutter/iOS) and returns application auth payload.

400.description: Missing or invalid Apple identity token.

get.description: Returns on/off preferences for push notifications, sponsorship alerts, and chat notifications.

patch.description: Updates one or more notification preferences. Omitted fields remain unchanged.

get.description: Returns all notifications for the authenticated user in descending order.

patch.description: Marks all unread notifications of the authenticated user as read.

200.description: All notifications marked as read (or no unread notifications).

patch.description: Marks a specific notification as read for the authenticated user.

400.description: Notification not found or unauthorized access.

patch.description: Marks a specific notification as unread for the authenticated user.

400.description: Notification not found or unauthorized access.

delete.description: Deletes a specific notification belonging to the authenticated user.

400.description: Notification not found or unauthorized access.

get.description: Returns the Strava OAuth authorization URL as JSON. Recommended for mobile apps and Swagger testing. Mobile client should open data.authUrl in system browser/in-app browser and wait for callback completion.
get.summary: Connect Strava - Get Strava authorization URL

get.description: Internal callback endpoint called by Strava after consent. Backend handles token exchange and connection persistence. Mobile/frontend usually does not call this directly; app only needs to handle post-callback success/error redirect screen.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: Authorization code returned by Strava after user consent.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Signed state created by backend to validate the callback request.

parameters.name: error
parameters.description: Error key returned by Strava when user denies consent.

get.description: Returns current Strava connection for authenticated user. Shows connection status, provider, provider user ID, scopes granted, token expiration, and sync history. Used to check connection validity before starting challenges.
get.summary: Get Strava connection status and details

delete.description: Revokes Strava connection for authenticated user. Disables automatic activity syncing. User must reconnect to participate in Strava-dependent challenges afterward. Existing synced activities remain in database for audit/history.

post.description: Pulls activities from Strava using stored tokens and upserts into synced activities table. Request body contains only optional filters/pagination, not activity payload. This endpoint can be called manually by app (on-demand sync). Optional automatic schedule is also available via env flags: STRAVA_AUTO_SYNC_ENABLED=true and STRAVA_AUTO_SYNC_CRON (default: */30 * * * *). Recommended first test body is {} or {"perPage":20,"maxPages":1}.

requestBody.description: Optional sync options only. Values are not taken from Strava payloads. Use GET /api/strava/connection to read externalConnectionId when needed.

get.description: Handles Strava webhook verification request (GET). Part of Strava webhook subscription setup flow. Called by Strava servers during subscription creation to verify callback URL ownership. Frontend/clients should NOT call this endpoint manually. Validates verify_token against STRAVA_WEBHOOK_VERIFY_TOKEN and echoes back challenge string.

parameters.name: hub.verify_token
parameters.description: Token sent by Strava. Must match environment variable STRAVA_WEBHOOK_VERIFY_TOKEN.

parameters.name: hub.challenge
parameters.description: Challenge string from Strava that must be echoed back in response.

200.description: Webhook verification successful. Challenge echoed back.

403.description: Webhook verify token does not match STRAVA_WEBHOOK_VERIFY_TOKEN.

post.description: Receives real-time Strava activity push events after webhook subscription is active. Called by Strava servers only when user completes/updates activities in Strava app. Frontend MUST NOT call this directly. Events trigger automatic activity sync and challenge progress projection rebuild. Supported events: activity.created (new activity), activity.updated (modified activity).

requestBody.description: Strava webhook event payload with updated activity metadata.

200.description: Webhook event accepted and queued for async processing.

post.summary: Receive Strava push events in real-time (webhook)

post.description: Creates Strava push subscription for real-time activity events. Requires STRAVA_WEBHOOK_CALLBACK_URL and STRAVA_WEBHOOK_VERIFY_TOKEN environment variables. Use for manual setup/recovery. Automatic one-time subscription bootstrap available via STRAVA_AUTO_WEBHOOK_SUBSCRIBE=true on backend startup. Strava UI does not provide webhook subscription form fields - must be created programmatically via this endpoint or Strava API.

200.description: Webhook subscription created successfully on Strava servers.

400.description: Missing STRAVA_WEBHOOK_CALLBACK_URL or STRAVA_WEBHOOK_VERIFY_TOKEN environment variables.

401.description: Missing or invalid admin access token.

get.description: Fetches list of active Strava push webhook subscriptions for this application. Use to verify subscription is set up and healthy. Useful after setup to confirm exactly one subscription exists and is correctly configured.

200.description: List of Strava webhook subscriptions fetched successfully.

401.description: Missing or invalid admin access token.

delete.description: Deletes a specific Strava webhook subscription by ID. Use for maintenance, decommissioning, or when rotating callback domain/verify token. After deletion, webhook events will no longer be received until new subscription is created.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Subscription ID from Strava (obtained from GET subscription endpoint)

401.description: Missing or invalid admin access token.

Returns details of one user by id.

patch.description: Bans or unbans an existing user by id.

delete.description: Deletes all notifications for current admin user.

get.description: Returns route plans with pagination. Optional filters by challengeId and search text.

parameters.name: challengeId
parameters.description: Filter route plans by challenge ID.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search by challenge title, route location, or about_challenge text.

get.description: Returns a single route plan by id including challenge info and day plans.

post.description: Creates a route plan for a specific challenge. This is used for challenges that have a route component, such as virtual adventures.

parameters.name: challengeId
parameters.description: Challenge id for which to create the route plan.

requestBody.description: Multipart payload for route plan creation. Upload `banner_image` to store route banner and provide `day_plans` as JSON array or object array.

about_challenge.example: A multi-day endurance route across mountain terrain designed for virtual adventure participants.

climate_terrain.example: Tropical rainforest, steep climbs, muddy tracks

description.example: Start from Owers Corner and move through gradual ascents with hydration points.

oneOf.example: [{"sequence":1,"day_number":"1-2 days","title":"Owers' Corner to Ua-Ule","description":"Start from Owers Corner","distance":"18km"}]

200.description: Route plan created successfully for the challenge.

post.summary: Create route plan for a challenge

patch.description: Updates an existing route plan. Optionally upload new banner_image to replace the existing one. To update day_plans: include `id` in items to update existing days, omit `id` to create new days, and days not in list are deleted.

requestBody.description: Multipart payload for route plan update. All fields are optional. If day_plans is provided, existing days are replaced.

about_challenge.example: Updated multi-day endurance route across mountain terrain.

banner_image.description: Optional new banner image file (jpeg/png/webp, max 5MB). If provided, replaces existing image.

id.description: Optional existing route day ID. If provided, updates that day; if omitted, creates new day.

oneOf.description: Array of day plans. Items with `id` update existing days; items without `id` create new days. Days not in list are deleted.
JSON stringified array (same structure as array above).

200.description: Route plan updated successfully. Day plans with `id` are updated; without `id` are created; omitted days are deleted.

400.description: Invalid route plan id or payload.

patch.summary: Update route plan for a challenge

get.summary: Get unread count for a conversation

post.summary: Add members to a group conversation (admin only)

patch.summary: Set role for a group member (admin only)

patch.description: Clears the full conversation history for the current user (from beginning up to now). This does not delete messages for other participants.
patch.summary: Clear all conversation history for current user only

patch.description: Only admins of a GROUP conversation can rename it. DM conversations are not supported.

400.description: Invalid title or conversation is not a group.

patch.description: Only admins of a GROUP conversation can update the group avatar. Accepts image file up to 5MB.

400.description: Invalid avatar file or conversation is not a group.

delete.description: Soft-deletes a conversation. For GROUP conversations, only admins can delete.

403.description: Not allowed to delete this conversation.

post.description: Creates a new support plan. planTypeId must come from GET /admin/support-plan/plan-types. Resource file (PDF/Document) is optional. Update the UI input filed based on the Plantype selected. For example, if the plan type is a running plan, show distance input; if it is a training plan, show training difficulty dropdown, etc.

description.example: A comprehensive 12-week training plan for 5km ultra running.

resource_url.description: PDF or document file for resource material (optional)

400.description: Invalid payload. Accepted file types: PDF, DOC, DOCX, TXT. Max size: 10MB. Category must be one of: RUNNING, CYCLING, SWIMMING, HIIT. TrainingPlansCategory must be one of: Beginner, Intermediate, Advanced.

patch.description: Update an existing support plan with the same fields as create. You can upload a new resource file (PDF/DOC/DOCX/TXT).

resource_url.description: PDF or document file for resource material (optional)

400.description: Invalid payload or support plan ID. Accepted file types: PDF, DOC, DOCX, TXT. Max size: 10MB.

delete.description: Delete a support plan by ID.

patch.description: Toggle support plan status (1 = active, 0 = inactive).

400.description: Invalid status or support plan ID.

get.description: Returns profile information for the authenticated admin user.

patch.description: Updates admin profile fields including name, email, bio, avatar. Avatar should be sent as multipart file.

get.summary: List messages in a conversation (cursor pagination)

post.description: Production flow: use this endpoint for messages in a conversation. For pure realtime text-only messages, use websocket event "message:send" on namespace "/ws". This endpoint creates and stores the message in DB and now also emits realtime "message:new" to the conversation room.
post.summary: Send a plain text message in a conversation

delete.description: Deletes a message by id. User can only delete messages they have permission to delete (e.g. their own messages, or any messages if they are admin).
delete.summary: Delete a message for current user permissions scope

post.description: Admin-only action for GROUP conversations. This blocks messaging for all current participants. After blocking, message sending is denied for both HTTP APIs and websocket realtime sends.

200.description: Conversation messaging blocked for all participants.

400.description: Only group conversations can be blocked.

403.description: Admin only or requester is not a member of this conversation.

post.description: User-side endpoint for creating only COMMUNITY_CHALLENGE submissions. Submission is saved as PENDING and stays inactive until reviewed by admin.

requestBody.description: Community challenge payload. Uses the same structure as admin community challenge create, but this endpoint always creates a pending submission.

200.description: Community challenge submitted successfully and waiting for admin approval. The challenge status will be PENDING and is_active will be false.

post.summary: Submit a community challenge for admin approval

get.description: Returns user-side challenge cards for one selected path only. Category can be used to narrow the selected path further. The response is shaped for the mobile screens and includes chat, leaderboard, and attempt action metadata.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search by challenge title or subtitle

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Optional category filter within the selected path

get.summary: Get challenge feed for user app

get.description: Returns comprehensive challenge information including title, description, metrics, checkpoints, user participation status, progress percentage, active checkpoint sequence, and chat metadata. Includes Strava connection requirements and leaderboard context for user positioning.

400.description: Challenge not found or has been deleted.

get.summary: Get challenge detail for user app

get.description: Returns ranked leaderboard for the specified challenge. Rankings based on: (1) completion status, (2) progress percentage, (3) finish_time_sec (excludes paused duration for fair comparison), (4) start time. Includes user profiles, metrics achieved, and your current position relative to other participants.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of top leaderboard rows to return (default: 50, max: 500)

200.description: Leaderboard fetched and ranked by completion criteria.

400.description: Challenge not found or leaderboard not available.

get.summary: Get challenge leaderboard with ranked standings

post.description: Registers user as a challenge participant with status=JOINED and sets joined_at timestamp. Does NOT start challenge timing (started_at remains null). Validates Strava connection requirement. User must call POST /challenges/:id/start separately when ready to begin timing. Optional externalConnectionId body parameter: if provided, uses that specific connection; if omitted, uses primary active Strava connection.

requestBody.description: Join request. externalConnectionId is optional - omit to use default Strava connection.

200.description: Successfully registered for challenge. Ready to call start endpoint.

400.description: Challenge not found, already in progress, requires Strava but not connected, or participation already exists.

post.summary: Join challenge - register as participant (NO timing start)

post.description: Begins challenge timing for an already-joined participation (status must be JOINED). Sets started_at timestamp and transitions status to IN_PROGRESS. Subsequent Strava activities with activity_date >= started_at are eligible for progress calculation. Activities during pause windows (logged challenge_paused/challenge_resumed events) are excluded. MUST be called after join endpoint. Empty body - uses Strava connection already selected/confirmed at join time.

200.description: Challenge started. Timing is now active. Ready for activities to be tracked.

400.description: Challenge not found, not joined first, already completed, Strava required but not connected, or invalid participation state.

post.summary: Start challenge timing - begin active attempt

post.description: Pauses an active IN_PROGRESS challenge. Sets status=PAUSED and logs challenge_paused event with timestamp. Strava activities with timestamps within pause window (between pause and resume) are automatically excluded from progress calculation. Pause duration is subtracted from final finish_time_sec for fair leaderboard ranking. Only callable when status=IN_PROGRESS. Call resume endpoint to continue from pause point with original started_at preserved.

200.description: Challenge paused successfully. Pause event logged. Activities during pause excluded.

400.description: Challenge not found, not in IN_PROGRESS status, already completed, or already paused.

post.summary: Pause challenge - suspend timer and exclude activities

post.description: Resumes a PAUSED challenge to IN_PROGRESS status. Logs challenge_resumed event with timestamp. Original started_at timestamp is preserved (NOT reset). Pause window is recorded in journey logs. Activities after resume continue counting toward progress. Pause duration automatically excluded from leaderboard finish_time_sec calculation for fair ranking. Only callable when status=PAUSED. Validates Strava connection still active if required by challenge.

200.description: Challenge resumed successfully. Pause window recorded. Ready for continued activity tracking.

400.description: Challenge not found, not in PAUSED status, Strava required but disconnected, or invalid participation state.

post.summary: Resume paused challenge - continue from pause point

delete.description: Abandons current challenge attempt and marks participation as ABANDONED. Removes user from challenge group conversation (if member). Can be called from any status (JOINED, IN_PROGRESS, PAUSED) except COMPLETED/ABANDONED. User can re-join using POST /challenges/:id/join endpoint afterward.

200.description: Left challenge successfully. Removed from group conversation.

400.description: Challenge not found, not joined, or already completed/abandoned.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of the authenticated user challenge participation records with related challenge data.

info.title: Limitless App API
info.description: Limitless App API documentation | Maintainer: Sazedul Islam | Contact: sazedul.bdcalling@gmail.com

refresh_token.description: The refresh token issued during login

identityToken.description: Apple identity token (JWT) from Flutter sign_in_with_apple credential.identityToken

email.description: Apple email (may be present only on first login)

pushNotifications.description: Master switch for general push notifications

externalConnectionId.description: Optional specific external connection id (must belong to authenticated user). Read this value from GET /api/strava/connection response.

after.description: Optional filter. Sync activities strictly after this UNIX timestamp (seconds). Example source: convert your target date/time to epoch seconds.

before.description: Optional filter. Sync activities strictly before this UNIX timestamp (seconds).

page.description: Optional pagination start page. Defaults to 1.

perPage.description: Optional pagination size. Strava max is 200. Practical test value is 20 or 50.

maxPages.description: Optional safety limit for pages in one request. Start with 1 for testing to avoid large backfills.

description.example: Complete this checkpoint to unlock next stage

display_name.description: Display name for the checkpoint (for map/UX)

challenge_kind.description: main_event creates a featured monthly campaign, benchmark creates repeatable performance tracking.

monthly_reset.description: For main_event this is enforced as true by backend. For benchmark this is used as provided.

metadata.description: main_event requires metadata.month_name. benchmark requires metadata.benchmark_group.

description.example: Complete 50km challenge based on path rules
Complete 50km challenge based on path rules
Complete 50km challenge based on path rules
Complete 50km challenge based on path rules
Complete 50km challenge based on path rules

externalConnectionId.description: Optional Strava external connection ID if user already has Strava connected

description.example: A challenge to run across Australia in 30 days

fundingGoal.description: The funding goal of the sponsorship

category.description: The category of the Challenge associated with the sponsorship

sponsorship_Needs.description: The needs associated with the sponsorship

sponsorshipId.description: The ID of the sponsorship to which the sponsor is being added

sponsorEmail.description: The contact email of the sponsor

sponsorPhone.description: The phone number of the sponsor

sponsorshipAmount.description: The sponsorship amount provided by the sponsor

sponsorMessage.description: Message or note from the sponsor
sponsorMessage.example: We are excited to support this cause!

status.description: New status for admin moderation flow

supportNeeded.example: Need help with organizing transport, food, crew support...

status.description: Admin status: pending, reviewed, contacted, completed
